config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar kekuasaan tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat kehidupan sosial di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara yang lemah atau minoritas. Masyarakat yang sehat seharusnya mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society’s interests should automatically outrank individual rights, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, censorship, and discrimination. Public safety and the common good matter, but they have to be pursued within firm limits that protect speech, due process, privacy, and equal treatment. If you let “society” become a blank check, minority rights and unpopular individuals are usually the first to suffer. To me, the better principle is that the public interest can justify some limits on individual freedom, but only in narrow, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembungkaman kritik, persekusi minoritas, atau keputusan sepihak dari pihak yang lebih kuat. Memang ada keadaan darurat atau kebutuhan publik tertentu yang bisa membatasi kebebasan individu, tetapi batasnya harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat justru mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk menindas orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that idea sounds too much like a shortcut to abuse, because history here shows that appeals to the "greater good" have often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, internment, and other violations of basic rights. Public safety and social order matter, but they should only limit individual rights in narrow, clearly defined situations, with strong legal safeguards and accountability. If society’s interests always come first, then minority rights and unpopular people become dangerously easy to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, menekan minoritas, atau melindungi kepentingan pihak yang lebih kuat, jadi saya tidak percaya itu bisa dibiarkan menjadi prinsip utama. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, terutama untuk keamanan dan ketertiban, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang mampu menjaga keseimbangan, bukan yang mengorbankan individu paling lemah atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From a U.S. perspective, that framing has too much historical baggage: it has been used to justify censorship, racial discrimination, internment, and other abuses that were later recognized as violations of basic rights. I also do not buy the idea that the “common good” is automatically more legitimate just because it is collective; majorities can be selfish, fearful, or wrong, and the people who pay the price are usually minorities or unpopular individuals. Society can and should place limited, clearly defined restrictions on individual freedom for real public needs, but rights have to be the default starting point, not something society can override whenever it finds it convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
